title: Chapter 1 – Prologue
content:
Prologue

It was a night when moonlight fell softly over the riverbank.

A man knocked at the estate gate.

His long ears and striking face were not of this world.

He called for the master of the estate, offering to stay the night as a guest and provide medical treatment in return.

Between the stranger's unearthly features and the scent of wild grass that clung to him, the gatekeeper did not dare turn him away.

As it happened, the master had a newborn child. He welcomed the long-eared man inside, intending to have the infant examined.

The man cradled the baby and studied it for a long moment before speaking.

"This one will struggle to live past twenty. It's a matter of constitution."

"An early death? This child was born after I disgraced its mother. Is this divine punishment?"

The man's expression soured at the master's words, as though he had heard something indecent.

"Is that what you ask a doctor? Even for an ignorant country bumpkin..."

"What about the child's condition?"

The master cleared his throat and pressed on.

"As a father, I'll tell you plainly. The condition resembles the Nine Yin Severed Pulse. I have never encountered anyone with a Baihui acupoint so extraordinarily open. It is as though the crown of the head were left wide apart."

"I thought the Nine Yin Severed Pulse killed through hardened meridians."

"You're not entirely ignorant, then. This child will die from meridians that are excessively open at the crown. The common term for it is ascension."

"Ascension? Isn't that when a Taoist master of great spiritual cultivation departs for the celestial realm?"

"You believe in such tales?"

The man stroked one of his long ears and continued.

"Perhaps you have heard of certain absolute masters who cultivate the upper Dantian of the head. Ascension is what happens when one becomes intoxicated by the energy of nature pouring down from the heavens — so overwhelmed that, in the end, they cannot endure it and cross the Sanzu River*. The celestial departure story is merely a distortion of that truth."

"Then it is divine punishment after all."

"You turtle of a man. And you call yourself a father..."

The man scowled and clicked his tongue. The master shot to his feet, jabbing a finger toward him.

"I've heard enough. You seem to be from the murim — what is your rank? Noble family or not, your words are deeply unpleasant."

"Rank? You dare speak of rank to me? Even if your great-grandfather's great-grandfather stood here before me..."

The man, now hurling insults at the master, was thrown out of the estate, and their conversation never spread beyond its walls.

* *

"Raised in an unorthodox sect, this child would have become a demon. Grown in Shaolin, the 72 arts would have doubled in their hands. But born a despised child in such a backwater place — there is no fate here but to die young in the countryside."

The man shook his head as he walked away.

"Unless they awaken on their own..."

Notes: Sanzu River — The Sanzu River is a mythological river that separates the world of the living from the afterlife.
